evals.prompt: I have an Angular component that renders a list of agents. There could be hundreds. The template currently uses: <div *ngFor="let agent of agents; trackBy: trackById">. I'm migrating it to the new Angular block syntax. Show me the migrated @for loop and explain what to use for track.
evals.expected_output: @for (agent of agents(); track agent.id) using agent.id (or agent.slug if no id). Explains that $index should not be used for API data. TrackBy function becomes inline track expression. agents() is likely a signal so called with (). Preserves OnPush compatibility automatically.

assertions.description: Uses track agent.id (or other stable identifier), not track $index
Explains or mentions that $index should not be used for API-sourced lists
Calls agents as a signal: agents() not just agents

evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) *ngFor missing trackBy/track, (2) getter filteredAgents recalculates every CD cycle — should be computed(), (3) getLabel() called per-item per-cycle — consider memoising or precomputing, (4) @Input() decorator is legacy — use input() signal API, (5) constructor injection is legacy — use inject(). Suggests migration to signal input + computed().

assertions.description: Flags *ngFor without trackBy as a performance issue
Identifies the getter filteredAgents as recalculating every CD cycle and suggests computed()
Notes @Input() decorator is legacy and suggests input() signal
Notes constructor injection is legacy and suggests inject()
Suggests computed() as the replacement for the getter

evals.prompt: I'm adding a new feature route to apps/dashboard. The component is DashboardTasksComponent. Show me how to add it to the routing file as a lazy-loaded route, and explain why it should be lazy.
evals.expected_output: Uses loadComponent: () => import('./tasks/dashboard-tasks.component').then(m => m.DashboardTasksComponent). Explains that eager loading increases initial bundle size. Does not suggest loadChildren + NgModule (component is standalone). Follows the existing dashboard app-routing.module.ts pattern.

assertions.description: Uses loadComponent with dynamic import, not component: DashboardTasksComponent (eager)
Does not suggest creating a new NgModule with loadChildren for a standalone component
Explains that lazy loading reduces initial bundle / improves initial load time
